skill.name: linear-milestones-write
skill.description: Create and update Linear project milestones with documented Bun CLI commands.
skill.summary: Manage Linear project milestones from a command-line workflow.
skill.author: Bind

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution detections are mostly Markdown examples for user-invoked Bun commands, not hidden runtime execution inside this package. The skill does require a Linear API key and describes write operations against Linear milestones, so publication should include a clear credential and external-service warning. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found in the audited files.

medium_findings.title: Linear API Key Required for Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill requires LINEAR_API_KEY in the environment or a git-root .env file and is intended to create or update Linear milestones. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it exposes a sensitive credential to CLI tooling and enables account-changing actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key requirement is explicitly declared in both files, and the skill describes write operations. I did not find evidence that the credential is exfiltrated.

medium_findings.title: Documented External CLI Commands Perform Writes
medium_findings.description: The documentation instructs users to run Bun commands that create and update milestones. These are user-invoked commands, so the static Ruby backtick detections are false positives, but the actions can modify Linear data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are clearly Markdown documentation rather than embedded executable code. Risk remains because the documented commands perform write actions against an external service.

medium_findings.title: Referenced Implementation Files Were Not Present
medium_findings.description: The manifest names TypeScript entry files, and the README examples refer to generated JavaScript command paths, but only skill.json and SKILL.md were present in this audit. The actual Linear write implementation could not be reviewed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file_structure shows only two audited files, while both audited files reference command implementations. This limits security confidence but does not prove malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is a Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the Bun runtime website in the prerequisites section. This is a benign documentation link, not a data transfer endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only as a Markdown prerequisite link. No evidence found of network code or credential transmission to that URL.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain ordinary milestone words and examples, such as Alpha, Beta, GA, and v1.0. No cryptographic function or hash algorithm use was found in the audited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The audited lines are prose or CLI option examples, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak algorithm usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Credential Loaded from Environment or .env
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill expects LINEAR_API_KEY from the environment or a git-root .env file. Users should scope the key carefully and avoid exposing it in shared project files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential source is explicitly documented. The pattern is risky because it handles secrets, but it is expected for a Linear integration.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Service Write Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented commands create and update Linear milestones. Mistaken inputs or over-permissive API keys could change project planning data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command sections explicitly cover create and update operations. The risk is operational rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Manage Linear Milestones from the CLI
content.value_statement: Planning teams need a repeatable way to create and revise Linear milestones without switching context. This skill documents Bun CLI commands that create and update milestones using a Linear API key.
actual_capabilities: Documents a create-milestone command with name and project inputs.
Documents an update-milestone command that accepts a milestone ID.
Supports milestone descriptions and target dates in the command examples.
Allows project selection by name or UUID according to the documentation.
Describes a JSON output flag for machine-readable command output.
limitations: Requires Bun and a valid LINEAR_API_KEY before commands can run.
Performs write operations that can change Linear project data.
Only skill.json and SKILL.md were present for this audit.
Referenced implementation files were not available for security review.

use_cases.title: Create release milestones
use_cases.description: Add named milestones with target dates for release planning.

use_cases.title: Update delivery dates
use_cases.description: Revise milestone names, descriptions, or target dates from a terminal workflow.

use_cases.title: Automate planning scripts
use_cases.description: Use JSON output to connect milestone changes with internal planning automation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a milestone
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Linear milestone named <name> for project <project> with target date <YYYY-MM-DD>.

prompt_templates.title: Update a milestone date
prompt_templates.prompt: Update Linear milestone <milestone-id> to target date <YYYY-MM-DD> and show the command before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Add milestone details
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Linear milestone for <project> named <name> with description <description> and target date <YYYY-MM-DD>.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare automation output
prompt_templates.prompt: Update milestone <milestone-id> with <changes> and return machine-readable output for a planning script.

output_examples.input: Create a milestone named Beta for the Monticello project with a February target date.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a Bun command that creates the Beta milestone for Monticello and includes the requested target date.

output_examples.input: Update milestone abc123 so the name is Beta Release.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares the update command for milestone abc123 with the new name and confirms it is a write operation.

output_examples.input: Create a milestone and return output suitable for automation.
output_examples.output: The assistant includes the JSON output option so downstream tooling can parse the result.

best_practices: Use a Linear API key with the minimum permissions needed for milestone changes.
Confirm project names and milestone IDs before running write commands.
Keep LINEAR_API_KEY out of shared repositories and command history.
anti_patterns: Do not run write commands against production projects without confirmation.
Do not store personal Linear API keys in committed .env files.
Do not assume partial project-name matching selected the intended project.

faq.question: What does this skill manage?
faq.answer: It documents commands for creating and updating Linear project milestones.

faq.question: Does it require a Linear API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation requires LINEAR_API_KEY in the environment or a git-root .env file.

faq.question: Can it delete milestones?
faq.answer: No delete command is documented in the audited files.

faq.question: Can it choose projects by name?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation says project names support case-insensitive partial matching.

faq.question: Does it support structured output?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented commands include a JSON output flag.

faq.question: Were the command implementations reviewed?
faq.answer: No. Only skill.json and SKILL.md were present in the audited package.
